summary: A React and TypeScript fan tool for Project Moon focused on literary origins, canto context, and cross-game lore connections. Now in Public Beta, the atlas features a comprehensive lore graph and a complete Universal Profile system.

milestones.name: Shared data layer
milestones.description: Define the TypeScript schema and ship the initial static data model used by the graph and panels.
deliverables: TypeScript types for Sinners, literary sources, themes, canto annotations, and entities
Static JSON data structure under src/data
Base Sinner dataset used by the graph

milestones.name: Lore graph MVP
milestones.description: Interactive D3 lore graph in React with detail panels and shared graph interactions.
deliverables: Cross-game entity node rendering and entity panel
Search entry point and graph selection flow

milestones.name: Lore graph full content
milestones.description: Expand the graph into a reliable reference tool with richer data, spoiler-aware canto context, source browsing, and consistency cleanup.
implemented_now: Canto annotations with spoiler gating in the UI
Filter panel for game, theme, and literary source
Shared literary group nodes in the graph
future_scope: Relationship Layering: Differentiate between primary vs. secondary literary sources with visual weight in the graph.

milestones.name: Universal profile cards
milestones.description: Richer polymorphic profile pages for Sinners, Sources, and Entities with deep-dives into lore, identities, EGOs, and timeline context.

milestones.name: Team builder (lore-aware)
milestones.description: Team composition tooling filtered by literary origin, theme, and affiliation rather than only combat mechanics.

milestones.name: Cross-game entity search polish
milestones.description: Improve search, deep links, and browse flows across Sinners, sources, and entity nodes.

milestones.name: Wings & NPC Expansion
milestones.description: Expand beyond Sinners to cover major NPCs and District-level lore (Indigo Elder, Dongrang, etc.) and their specific literary roots (Hemingway, Kafka-parallelism).
deliverables: New entity nodes for District NPCs and secondary antagonists
Deep-dive sources for Dreaming Electric Sheep (PKD) and Indigo Elder (Hemingway)
Expanded 'Secondary Source' mapping for complex characters like Erlkönig Heathcliff

next_action: Proceed to M5: Team Builder — leveraging the literary and thematic connections now surfaced in the Profile System to filter composition groups.

notes: README and roadmap were refreshed to match the implemented repo state, including About/Roadmap pages and supplemental data files.
M1, M2, M3 and M4 are complete.
M7 is in progress following community feedback integration for literary influences.
